briefs.title: BC creates new power supply rules for AI data centres and hydrogen production facilities
briefs.summary: BC's new Data Centre Facility and Hydrogen Production Facility Power Supply Regulation sets out how AI data centres, conventional data centres, and hydrogen-for-export facilities can connect to BC Hydro's electricity grid. It defines what counts as an AI data centre (a facility using 10% or more of its power for artificial intelligence tasks) and establishes a formal process — including design deposits (upfront payments to cover the cost of planning grid upgrades) — for these facilities to request electricity capacity.
briefs.action_required: If you operate or plan to open an AI data centre or hydrogen facility in BC, review the new connection process and deposit requirements now.

key_dates.label: BC Data Centre & Hydrogen Facility Power Supply Regulation deposit date
